name: did-hosting-server
description: DID-hosting node that talks DIDComm via a shared mediator and exposes no public HTTP endpoint. Use this for a witness, watcher, or any service whose role is consumed via DIDComm rather than an inbound URL. The DID document carries a single DIDCommMessaging service pointing at the mediator. URL or WEBVH_SERVER is still required at provision time so the VTA knows where to publish the did:webvh log itself, but neither appears in the rendered document. Hosts that publish DID logs over HTTP should use 'did-hosting-daemon'; hosts that need both should use 'did-hosting-control'.
